modules.name: aiecsjs
modules.description: Core entry point — world, entity, component, query, system, utilities.

modules.name: aiecsjs/commands
modules.description: Command buffer for deferred structural mutation during iteration.

modules.name: aiecsjs/observers
modules.description: Reactive observers for component add/remove/set events.

modules.name: aiecsjs/relations
modules.description: Entity relations and hierarchies. Stable since 0.4.0; the graph API and built-in ChildOf relation are frozen for the 1.x track.

exports.name: createWorld
exports.signature: function createWorld(options?: WorldOptions): World
exports.summary: Create a new ECS world.

params.name: options
params.description: Configuration; all fields have defaults. See WorldOptions type.

returns.description: Opaque world handle passed to all other API calls.

examples.title: SAB-backed world
examples.code: const world = createWorld({ buffer: new SharedArrayBuffer(64*1024*1024) })

exports.name: destroyWorld
exports.signature: function destroyWorld(world: World): void
exports.summary: Release all internal buffers; the world reference becomes invalid. Use `disposeWorld` in new code; this alias is retained for backwards compatibility and is scheduled for removal in 1.0.

exports.name: disposeWorld
exports.signature: function disposeWorld(world: World): void
exports.summary: Release all internal buffers; the world reference becomes invalid. Aligns with the ai*js ecosystem `dispose()` convention (aifsmjs Runtime, aibridgejs Bridge).

exports.name: resetWorld
exports.signature: function resetWorld(world: World): void
exports.summary: Wipe entities and components but keep the world allocated. Useful for hot module reload.

exports.name: getWorldSize
exports.signature: function getWorldSize(world: World): number
exports.summary: Number of alive entities in the world.

exports.name: getWorldCapacity
exports.signature: function getWorldCapacity(world: World): number
exports.summary: Current upper bound on entity slot count. May grow if maxEntities allows.

exports.name: createEntity
exports.signature: function createEntity(world: World): EntityId
exports.summary: Allocate a fresh entity. Returns a versioned 32-bit ID.

exports.name: destroyEntity
exports.signature: function destroyEntity(world: World, eid: EntityId): void
exports.summary: Remove all components, bump generation, free slot.

exports.name: entityExists
exports.signature: function entityExists(world: World, eid: EntityId): boolean
exports.summary: True iff the entity's index is allocated AND its generation matches.

exports.name: getEntityIndex
exports.signature: function getEntityIndex(eid: EntityId): number
exports.summary: Extract the index portion of a packed entity ID.

exports.name: getEntityGeneration
exports.signature: function getEntityGeneration(eid: EntityId): number
exports.summary: Extract the generation portion of a packed entity ID. Uses default 8-bit generation layout. For non-default `createWorld({ generationBits })`, use `EntityRef` + `deref` instead.

exports.name: packEntity
exports.signature: function packEntity(index: number, generation: number): EntityId
exports.summary: Pack an index and generation into an EntityId using default 24/8 bit layout. For non-default `createWorld({ indexBits, generationBits })`, use `EntityRef` + `deref` instead.

exports.name: defineComponent
exports.signature: function defineComponent<S extends SoASchema>(schema: S): SoAComponent<S>
exports.summary: Declare a Structure-of-Arrays component. Each field becomes a TypedArray column indexed by entity ID.

params.name: schema
params.description: Map of field name → field type. Field type is a member of Types, or [type, vectorLength] for fixed-size vectors.

examples.title: Position with two scalars
examples.code: const Position = defineComponent({ x: Types.f32, y: Types.f32 })

examples.title: Transform with vector field
examples.code: const Transform = defineComponent({ pos: [Types.f32, 3], scale: Types.f32 })

exports.name: defineTag
exports.signature: function defineTag(): TagComponent
exports.summary: Declare a zero-byte tag component. No fields.

exports.name: defineObjectComponent
exports.signature: function defineObjectComponent<T>(factory?: () => T): AoSComponent<T>
exports.summary: Declare an Array-of-Structures component. Each entity stores a separate JS object. Main-thread only.

params.name: factory
params.description: Default value factory invoked ONCE at definition time. Mutate per-entity instances via setComponent/getComponent.

examples.title: Reference to a Three.js mesh
examples.code: const MeshRef = defineObjectComponent<{ mesh: any }>(() => ({ mesh: null }))

exports.name: addComponent
exports.signature: function addComponent<C extends ComponentLike>(world: World, eid: EntityId, component: C, initial?: unknown): void
exports.summary: Attach a component to an entity. Argument order is (world, eid, component, init) — different from bitECS.

params.name: initial
params.description: Initial values; shape depends on component kind.

throws.when: eid does not exist (dev mode only).

exports.name: removeComponent
exports.signature: function removeComponent<C extends ComponentLike>(world: World, eid: EntityId, component: C): void
exports.summary: Detach a component from an entity. Triggers archetype migration.

exports.name: hasComponent
exports.signature: function hasComponent<C extends ComponentLike>(world: World, eid: EntityId, component: C): boolean
exports.summary: Test whether an entity has a component.

exports.name: getComponent
exports.signature: function getComponent<C extends ComponentLike>(world: World, eid: EntityId, component: C): ComponentView<C>
exports.summary: Retrieve a component view. For SoA, returns the archetype's column map; index by eid. For AoS, returns the per-entity object.

examples.title: Read SoA value
examples.code: const pos = getComponent(world, e, Position); console.log(pos.x[e], pos.y[e])

exports.name: setComponent
exports.signature: function setComponent<C extends ComponentLike, V>(world: World, eid: EntityId, component: C, value: V): void
exports.summary: Bulk-write component fields. For SoA, value is Partial<{field: value}>. For AoS, replaces the per-entity object's fields.

exports.name: defineQuery
exports.signature: function defineQuery(descriptor: ComponentLike[] | QueryDescriptor): Query
exports.summary: Create a reusable query selector. The same component set always returns the same Query reference within a module.

params.name: descriptor
params.description: Shorthand component list (treated as { all: [...] }) or a full QueryDescriptor with all/any/none arrays.

throws.when: any descriptor argument is not a Component.

examples.title: Full descriptor with exclusions
examples.code: const visibleEnemies = defineQuery({ all: [Position, Sprite, Enemy], none: [Hidden] })

exports.name: runQuery
exports.signature: function runQuery(world: World, query: Query): readonly EntityId[]
exports.summary: Return matching entities as a frozen array. Allocates each call; not for hot loops.

exports.name: forEachEntity
exports.signature: function forEachEntity<Q extends Query>(world: World, query: Q, fn: (eid: EntityId, ...columns: any[]) => void): void
exports.summary: Hot iteration path. Component column views are passed in declaration order after the entity ID.

params.name: fn
params.description: Per-entity callback. Receives eid then component column views in declaration order.

examples.title: Update positions
examples.code: forEachEntity(world, movers, (e, pos, vel) => { pos.x[e] += vel.x[e]; pos.y[e] += vel.y[e] })

exports.name: iterQuery
exports.signature: function iterQuery(world: World, query: Query): IterableIterator<EntityId>
exports.summary: Iterator over matching entity IDs. Allocates an iterator; use forEachEntity in hot loops.

exports.name: enterQuery
exports.signature: function enterQuery(query: Query): Query
exports.summary: Reactive query that yields only entities newly matching this frame.

exports.name: exitQuery
exports.signature: function exitQuery(query: Query): Query
exports.summary: Reactive query that yields only entities that left the match this frame.

exports.name: queryArchetypes
exports.signature: function queryArchetypes(world: World, query: Query): readonly Archetype[]
exports.summary: Power-user access to matching archetype tables. For custom iteration, GPU upload, or profiling.

examples.title: Per-archetype upload
examples.code: for (const a of queryArchetypes(world, q)) { /* a.size, a.mask */ }

exports.name: pipe
exports.signature: function pipe<W extends World, Ctx>(...systems: System<W, Ctx>[]): System<W, Ctx>
exports.summary: Compose systems left-to-right. Associative; always returns the same World reference.

examples.title: Frame tick
examples.code: const tick = pipe(input, physics, render); tick(world, dt)

exports.name: createLoop
exports.signature: function createLoop(options: LoopOptions): { start(): void; stop(): void }
exports.summary: Fixed-timestep accumulator loop. Calls onUpdate with constant dt; onRender once per animation frame with an interpolation alpha.

examples.title: 60Hz loop
examples.code: const loop = createLoop({ fixed: 1/60, onUpdate: dt => tick(world, dt) }); loop.start()

exports.name: createCommandBuffer
exports.signature: function createCommandBuffer(world: World): CommandBuffer
exports.summary: Create a buffer that queues structural mutations until flushed.

exports.name: flush
exports.signature: function flush(cb: CommandBuffer): void
exports.summary: Apply all queued operations to the world.

exports.name: withCommandBuffer
exports.signature: function withCommandBuffer<R>(world: World, fn: (cb: CommandBuffer) => R): R
exports.summary: Scoped command buffer; auto-flushes at end of callback. Forwards the return value.

examples.title: Safe destroy in iter
examples.code: withCommandBuffer(world, cb => { forEachEntity(world, dying, e => cb.destroy(e)) })

exports.name: observe
exports.signature: function observe(world: World, query: Query, event: 'add' | 'remove' | 'set', handler: (eid: EntityId) => void, opts?: ObserverOptions): () => void
exports.summary: General-purpose query observer. Returns a disposer. `opts.signal` (since 0.2.0) auto-unsubscribes when aborted.

exports.name: onAdd
exports.signature: function onAdd(world: World, component: ComponentLike, handler: (eid: EntityId) => void, opts?: ObserverOptions): () => void
exports.summary: Listen for component-add events on a specific component. `opts.signal` (since 0.2.0) auto-unsubscribes when aborted.

exports.name: onRemove
exports.signature: function onRemove(world: World, component: ComponentLike, handler: (eid: EntityId) => void, opts?: ObserverOptions): () => void
exports.summary: Listen for component-remove events. `opts.signal` (since 0.2.0) auto-unsubscribes when aborted.

exports.name: onSet
exports.signature: function onSet<C extends ComponentLike>(world: World, component: C, handler: (eid: EntityId, value: unknown) => void, opts?: ObserverOptions): () => void
exports.summary: Low-level mutation hook — fires after `setComponent` when the component is already present on the entity (an `addComponent` followed by `setComponent` fires onAdd then onSet). NOT a reactive value-predicate query. `opts.signal` (since 0.2.0) auto-unsubscribes when aborted.

exports.name: serializeWorld
exports.signature: function serializeWorld(world: World, options?: SerializeOptions): Uint8Array
exports.summary: Produce a versioned binary snapshot of the world.

exports.name: deserializeWorld
exports.signature: function deserializeWorld(bytes: Uint8Array, options?: DeserializeOptions): World
exports.summary: Restore a world from binary bytes.

exports.name: toJSON
exports.signature: function toJSON(world: World): WorldSnapshot
exports.summary: Produce a human-readable JSON snapshot. Slower than serializeWorld; inspectable in DevTools.

exports.name: fromJSON
exports.signature: function fromJSON(snapshot: WorldSnapshot): World
exports.summary: Restore from a JSON snapshot.

exports.name: createDeltaSerializer
exports.signature: function createDeltaSerializer(world: World, options?: SerializeOptions): DeltaSerializer
exports.summary: Capture only changes since the previous capture. For network replication. Wire format may change before 1.0.

examples.title: Send delta over WS
examples.code: const tx = createDeltaSerializer(world, { components: [Position] }); setInterval(() => ws.send(tx.capture()), 50)

exports.name: transferableSnapshot
exports.signature: function transferableSnapshot(world: World): { buffer: SharedArrayBuffer; meta: WorldMeta }
exports.summary: Package a SAB-backed world for postMessage to a Worker.

exports.name: adoptSnapshot
exports.signature: function adoptSnapshot(snap: { buffer: SharedArrayBuffer; meta: WorldMeta }): World
exports.summary: On the Worker side, materialise a world from a snapshot sent over postMessage.

returns.description: Worker-side world view backed by the same SAB.

exports.name: attachWorld
exports.signature: function attachWorld(buffer: SharedArrayBuffer, options?: { readOnly?: boolean }): World
exports.summary: Attach to a SAB that was set up by another thread. Use readOnly when this thread should only read.

exports.name: detachWorld
exports.signature: function detachWorld(world: World): void
exports.summary: Release this thread's view of a shared world. Does NOT free the SAB.

exports.name: defineRelation
exports.signature: function defineRelation<T = void>(options?: { exclusive?: boolean }): Relation<T>
exports.summary: Declare a relation type between two entities. Pass `exclusive: true` for one-to-one relations (e.g. ChildOf).

params.name: options
params.description: If exclusive, each source may relate to at most one target.

exports.name: addRelation
exports.signature: function addRelation<T>(world: World, source: EntityId, rel: Relation<T>, target: EntityId, data?: T): void
exports.summary: Add a relation edge from source to target.

examples.title: Mark Alice as child of Parent

exports.name: removeRelation
exports.signature: function removeRelation(world: World, source: EntityId, rel: Relation, target: EntityId): void
exports.summary: Remove a single relation edge.

exports.name: getRelationTargets
exports.signature: function getRelationTargets(world: World, source: EntityId, rel: Relation): readonly EntityId[]
exports.summary: Enumerate targets reachable from source by this relation.

exports.name: getRelationData
exports.signature: function getRelationData<T>(world: World, source: EntityId, rel: Relation<T>, target: EntityId): T | undefined
exports.summary: Read the data payload attached to a relation edge. Returns the value passed as `data` to `addRelation`, or `undefined` if no such edge exists or no data was stored. Subject to raw slot-keying: edges are keyed by slot index, not generation; validate liveness with `entityExists` if slot recycling is a concern.

examples.title: Read relation payload
examples.code: const data = getRelationData(world, alice, Likes, bob) // { since: 2024 } or undefined

exports.name: isWorld
exports.signature: function isWorld(x: unknown): x is World
exports.summary: Type guard for World values.

exports.name: isEntity
exports.signature: function isEntity(world: World, x: unknown): x is EntityId
exports.summary: Type guard for live EntityId in the given world.

exports.name: refOf
exports.signature: function refOf<T = unknown>(world: World, entity: EntityId): EntityRef<T>
exports.summary: Create an ABA-safe reference to a live entity. Throws EntityNotAliveError if the entity is dead or invalid.

returns.description: Frozen ref object with id and worldId.

throws.when: entity is dead, never created, or eid === 0.

exports.name: deref
exports.signature: function deref<T = unknown>(world: World, ref: EntityRef<T>): EntityId | null
exports.summary: Resolve a ref to its live EntityId, or null if stale / cross-world / OOB. Never throws.

returns.description: Live entity id, or null if stale.

examples.title: Guard clause pattern
examples.code:
const live = deref(world, ref)
if (live !== null) { /* entity is alive */ }

exports.name: aliveRef
exports.signature: function aliveRef<T = unknown>(world: World, ref: EntityRef<T>): boolean
exports.summary: Boolean guard form of deref. Returns true if ref is alive, false otherwise. Never throws.

returns.description: true if entity is still alive.

exports.name: EntityNotAliveError
exports.signature: class EntityNotAliveError extends Error { readonly eid: number }
exports.summary: Thrown by refOf when the entity is not alive. deref and aliveRef never throw.

examples.title: Catch specific error
examples.code: try { refOf(world, staleEid) } catch (e) { if (e instanceof EntityNotAliveError) { /* handle */ } }

types.name: EntityRef
types.definition: interface EntityRef<T = unknown> { readonly id: EntityId; readonly worldId: number; readonly __phantom?: T }

types.name: WorldOptions
types.definition: type WorldOptions = { initialCapacity?: number; maxEntities?: number; indexBits?: 20 | 24; generationBits?: 8 | 12 | 16; buffer?: SharedArrayBuffer; bufferByteOffset?: number }

types.name: World
types.definition: interface World { readonly id: number; readonly capacity: number; readonly version: string }

types.name: EntityId
types.definition: type EntityId = number & { readonly __brand: 'EntityId' }

types.name: SoASchema
types.definition: type SoASchema = Record<string, SoAFieldDecl>

types.name: SoAComponent
types.definition: interface SoAComponent<S extends SoASchema> { readonly __schema: S; readonly __soa: true }

types.name: AoSComponent
types.definition: interface AoSComponent<T> { readonly __aos: true }

types.name: TagComponent
types.definition: interface TagComponent { readonly __tag: true }

types.name: ComponentLike
types.definition: type ComponentLike = SoAComponent<any> | AoSComponent<any> | TagComponent

types.name: QueryDescriptor
types.definition: type QueryDescriptor = { all?: ComponentLike[]; any?: ComponentLike[]; none?: ComponentLike[] }

types.name: Query
types.definition: interface Query { readonly id: number; readonly mask: ReadonlyArray<number> }

types.name: System
types.definition: type System<W extends World = World, Ctx = unknown> = (world: W, ctx: Ctx) => W

types.name: LoopOptions
types.definition: type LoopOptions = { fixed?: number; maxSubSteps?: number; onUpdate: (dt: number) => void; onRender?: (alpha: number) => void }

types.name: CommandBuffer
types.definition: interface CommandBuffer { add<C>(eid: EntityId, c: C, init?: unknown): void; remove<C>(eid: EntityId, c: C): void; destroy(eid: EntityId): void; create(): EntityId }

types.name: SerializeOptions
types.definition: type SerializeOptions = { components?: ComponentLike[] }

types.name: DeserializeOptions
types.definition: type DeserializeOptions = { onUnknownVersion?: 'throw' | 'best-effort' }

types.name: WorldSnapshot
types.definition: interface WorldSnapshot { /* opaque, JSON-serializable */ }

types.name: DeltaSerializer
types.definition: interface DeltaSerializer { capture(): Uint8Array; apply(world: World, delta: Uint8Array): void; reset(): void }

types.name: Archetype
types.definition: interface Archetype { readonly id: number; readonly mask: ReadonlyArray<number>; readonly size: number }

types.name: Relation
types.definition: interface Relation<T = void> { readonly __relation: true }

constants.name: VERSION
constants.description: Equals the published npm package version.

constants.name: IS_SAB_SUPPORTED
constants.description: True iff SharedArrayBuffer is available in the current runtime.

constants.name: Types
constants.description: Map of SoA field type identifiers: i8 u8 i16 u16 i32 u32 f32 f64 eid bool.

constants.name: ChildOf
constants.description: Built-in exclusive parent-child relation. Each entity may have at most one parent at a time.
